prevalence.trend: Increasing by 0.2% annually due to aging population

incidence.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2019 (IHME), adapted for Brazil

vulnerablePopulations: Individuals in rural Northeast and Centro-Oeste regions
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Diagnosis based on 12-lead ECG showing irregular RR intervals with no discernible P waves, per ESC 2020 guidelines adapted by SBC

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 45% rate of anticoagulation in diagnosed cases

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Standard ECG and Holter monitoring, with emphasis on Chagas screening in endemic areas
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Rate/rhythm control per SBC guidelines; anticoagulation with DOACs or warfarin via SUS
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access in rural areas; high Chagas prevalence complicating management
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust SUS coverage for urban centers; national Chagas surveillance program aiding early detection
